title: Crunchy Syrian Chicken Kebabs
description: Garlicky and crunchy chicken kebabs marinated in a honeysugarspice blend, finished with a sweet glaze and crispy almondsesame coating-a showoff Syrian dish that stays ketofriendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 200 °C (400 °F) and prepare skewers.
In a bowl, combine chicken, olive oil, garlic, cumin, coriander, paprika, sumac, salt, and pepper. Mix well and let marinate for at least 15 minutes.
While the chicken marinates, warm the honey over low heat until slightly runny; set aside to use as glaze.
In a separate bowl, mix almond flour and toasted sesame seeds.
Thread the marinated chicken onto skewers, pressing firmly to secure.
Heat a skillet over medium‑high heat and sear the skewers for 2–3 minutes on each side until lightly browned.
Transfer the skewers to a baking sheet, brush with the honey glaze, then sprinkle the almond‑sesame mixture over the top.
Bake in the preheated oven for 10–12 minutes, or until the chicken reaches an internal temperature of 75 °C (165 °F) and the coating is crisp.
Remove from the oven, let rest for 5 minutes, then optionally drizzle remaining honey glaze and a pinch of sumac for extra flavor.
Serve hot, optionally garnished with fresh parsley.
